perturbation.original_text: 5. Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order, provided that t he Recipient notifies the Owner so that an adequate opp ortunit y is gi ven to respon d to such order.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the clause in Section 5(e) that requires the Recipient to notify the Owner when proprietary information is disclosed pursuant to a judicial or governmental order, allowing the Owner an opportunity to respond. The change creates an in-text contradiction because, without this notification requirement, the Owner may be unaware of the disclosure, hindering their ability to protect their proprietary information, which is a core intent of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligatio ns of parties under this Ag reemen t shall not appl y to i nform ation which: a. was alread y in the publi c domain when disclosed; or sub sequent ly falls into t he public d omain other than throug h disclosure by the Recipient; or disclosed by the R ecipient with the written approval of the Owner; b. was alread y in the possession of or known by the Recipient free of any obligation at the time disclosed to Recipient; c. beco mes known to the Recipient from a source o ther than the Owner with out breach of this Agreement by the Recipient; d. is develope d independen tly by the Recipient withou t use of the Owner’s Propri etary Inform ation; or e. is disclosed p ursuant t o judicial or governm ental order.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5(e) included a requirement for the Recipient to notify the Owner of any disclosure pursuant to a judicial or governmental order. The changed text removes this notification requirement. The contradicted text is the original text. By removing the notification requirement, the changed text creates a contradiction because it hinders the Owner's ability to protect their proprietary information. The changed text fails to provide the Owner with the chance to respond to such order, contradicting the intention of protecting the Owner's proprietary information. Therefore, it creates an in-text contradiction according to the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Agreement will bind both parties’ affiliates, subsid iaries, successors an d assigns. Recipient cer tifies that no Proprietary Inform ation, or any part of it will be exported to any countr y in violation of the export laws and regulations of the United States government. If any portion of this Agreem ent is deter mined to be invali d or unenfor ceable, th e remainder wi ll be in effect . Failure by either part y to en force any provision of this Agreem ent will not be deem ed a waiver of future enforcement of that or any other pr ovisi on. Any m odification s to this Agree ment must be made in writing and must be signed by both part ies. This Agreement w ill be governed by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Agreement will bind both parties’ affiliates, subsid iaries, successors an d assigns. Recipient cer tifies that no Proprietary Inform ation, or any part of it will be exported to any countr y in violation of the export laws and regulations of the United States government. If any portion of this Agreem ent is deter mined to be invali d or unenfor ceable, th e remainder wi ll be in effect . Failure by either part y to en force any provision of this Agreem ent will be deem ed a waiver of future enforcement of that or any other pr ovisi on. Any m odification s to this Agree ment must be made verbally and agreed by both parties. This Agreement w ill be governed by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 6 by changing the requirement for modifications to the agreement. The original text stipulates that any modifications must be made in writing and signed by both parties. The modified text states that modifications must be made verbally and agreed upon by both parties. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the acceptable method for modifying the agreement, rendering both clauses questionable in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will bind both parties’ affiliates, subsid iaries, successors an d assigns. Recipient cer tifies that no Proprietary Inform ation, or any part of it will be exported to any countr y in violation of the export laws and regulations of the United States government. If any portion of this Agreem ent is deter mined to be invali d or unenfor ceable, th e remainder wi ll be in effect . Failure by either part y to en force any provision of this Agreem ent will be deem ed a waiver of future enforcement of that or any other pr ovisi on. Any m odification s to this Agree ment must be made verbally and agreed by both parties. This Agreement w ill be governed by the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces a direct contradiction within Section 6. The original text requires modifications to be in writing and signed, while the changed text requires them to be verbal and agreed upon. This creates an irreconcilable conflict regarding the method of modifying the agreement, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, as the contradiction resides within the same section after the modification. Since the original text and change text create a direct contradiction to each other, it follows the "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction" definition.

perturbation.original_text: 2. “Owner” means the party who discloses Propr ietary Inform ation. “Recipient” means the party who receiv es Proprietary Inform ation of or from the Owner. Owner warr ants that it has the right to disclose the Proprietary Inform ation to Recipient. All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" without an y warranty regarding its a ccuracy or performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors. No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation are granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. “Owner” means the party who discloses Propr ietary Inform ation. “Recipient” means the party who receiv es Proprietary Inform ation of or from the Owner. Owner warr ants that it has the right to disclose the Proprietary Inform ation to Recipient. All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" with full warranty regarding its accuracy and performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors. No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation are granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 2 by changing 'without any warranty regarding its accuracy or performance' to 'with full warranty regarding its accuracy and performance'. The original text states that the proprietary information is provided "AS IS" without any warranty. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating that the information is provided with a full warranty. This introduces confusion regarding the owner's liability for the accuracy and performance of the proprietary information, since both clauses are in force.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Owner” means the party who discloses Propr ietary Inform ation. “Recipient” means the party who receiv es Proprietary Inform ation of or from the Owner. Owner warr ants that it has the right to disclose the Proprietary Inform ation to Recipient. All inform ation is pro vided "AS IS" with full warranty regarding its accuracy and performance and will remain the pr operty of the O wner and/or its licensors. No license or other ri ghts to Proprietary Inform ation are granted or implied under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states "All information is pro vided 'AS IS' without any warranty regarding its a ccuracy or performance." The changed text modifies this to "All inform ation is pro vided 'AS IS' with full warranty regarding its accuracy and performance." This creates a direct contradiction because the original explicitly disclaims any warranty, while the changed text provides a full warranty, making them irreconcilable.
